steps.title: Set the equations equal
steps.content:
Intersection points have the same $y$-value on both graphs.
$$bx^2-d=b+d$$

steps.title: Count the intersections
steps.content: Since $b>0$ and $d>0$, the value $\frac{b+2d}{b}$ is positive. An equation of the form $x^2=k$ with $k>0$ has two distinct real solutions. The answer is C.
